@@ -647,6 +647,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-dn1hf","title":"[HIGH] ffs-core btrfs root-tree and readdir tests now fail on CRC32C checksum mismatch","description":"# Review finding

Workspace gate `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` currently fails 10 `ffs-core` tests that all unwrap the same parser error: `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`.

## Failed tests
- `tests::open_fs_from_btrfs_image`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_debug_format`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_superblock_accessor`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree`
- `tests::walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`

## Failure sites
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22693`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22711`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22723`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22736`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23342`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23365`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23388`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23409`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23442`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23489`

## Why this is high severity
This is a workspace-red regression in core synthetic btrfs coverage. It breaks basic `OpenFs::from_device()` and `readdir()` expectations for internal fixtures, so new btrfs behavior cannot be trusted until the checksum stamping / parser-validation mismatch is resolved.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Audit recent btrfs fixture builders or validation-path changes so synthetic root-tree and dir-index blocks have checksums stamped consistently with current parser verification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:47:51.099879377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:34:08.975670287Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:34:08.973372486Z","close_reason":"Already shipped; targeted ffs-core root-tree and readdir repros now pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}
 {"id":"bd-dnet8","title":"add mounted-path ext4 hard-link unlink nlink-accounting coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:55:50.955797044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dofky","title":"add mounted-path ext4 symlink occupied-destination EEXIST and non-directory-parent ENOTDIR coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path FUSE E2E proves symlink create/readlink success, but it does not freeze the public refusal contract for creating a symlink over an existing name or beneath a regular-file parent even though ffs-core already returns Exists and NotADirectory. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes that assert exact EEXIST for occupied destinations and ENOTDIR for non-directory parents, with no stray entries or inode leaks after rejection. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes ext4 symlink creation refusal behavior for existing names and non-directory parents.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:28:22.355545253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-dqogl","title":"Reject unsupported btrfs extent encoding fields","description":"parse_extent_data reads the btrfs extent_data compression byte but silently ignores nonzero encryption and other_encoding fields. Unsupported encoded extents can then be treated as raw or merely compressed data. Reject nonzero encryption/other_encoding during extent parsing, add parser regressions, and update coverage notes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T13:23:49.708369591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T13:31:31.487971330Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T13:31:31.485859941Z","close_reason":"Rejected unsupported btrfs EXTENT_DATA encryption and other_encoding fields during parsing; added adversarial parser regressions and updated parity/coverage docs. Validation passed: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, focused ffs-btrfs extent parser tests, ffs-btrfs adversarial boundary test, UBS touched file, ffs-btrfs package check, and ffs-btrfs package clippy. Workspace check/clippy were attempted but are currently blocked by unrelated dirty ffs-fuse edits missing FsOps methods and a range_plus_one clippy finding.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-du5a","title":"Align AGENTS/README architecture notes with current crate graph","description":"Fix documented drifts discovered during archaeology: (1) clarify integration test placement (crate-local vs workspace tests/ fixtures), (2) update ffs-btrfs role wording to reflect active dependency in ffs-core, and (3) clarify ffs facade scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:13:31.344879246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T06:14:13.518811866Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T06:14:13.518789944Z","close_reason":"Updated AGENTS/README to match current test layout and crate roles (ffs facade + ffs-btrfs)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","docs"]}
 {"id":"bd-e270t","title":"Freeze exact AtomicMetrics debug contract","description":"Tighten the AtomicMetrics debug-format test from substring checks to an exact golden assertion in crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T03:51:56.494013472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T03:56:52.615074089Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T03:56:52.612836163Z","close_reason":"Added exact AtomicMetrics debug golden contract; verified with focused rch test plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","testing-golden-artifacts"]}
 {"id":"bd-e3oq","title":"Fix tracing callsite interest cache poisoning in log contract tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:49:38.035842044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:49:38.224954353Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T03:49:38.224676818Z","close_reason":"Fixed: use Dispatch+set_default+rebuild_interest_cache, serialize tests calling tracing-emitting functions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -48,7 +48,7 @@
 | btrfs internal node parsing | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `parse_internal_items` in `ffs-ondisk` |
 | btrfs sys_chunk mapping | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c` | ✅ | `map_logical_to_physical` in `ffs-ondisk`, with proptest + metamorphic coverage in `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` for translation covariance, chunk-order permutation invariance, and Single-vs-DUP primary mapping equivalence. |
 | btrfs read-only tree walk | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `walk_tree` in `ffs-btrfs` |
-| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. |
+| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported nonzero extent encryption and other-encoding fields instead of treating encoded payloads as raw readable data. |
 | btrfs open/validate pipeline | `fs/btrfs/disk-io.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsContext` in `ffs-core` |
 | btrfs transaction parity | `fs/btrfs/transaction.c` | ✅ | Transaction begin/commit/abort semantics and integration paths are implemented with coverage in unit + E2E suites. |
 | btrfs delayed refs parity | `fs/btrfs/delayed-ref.c` | ✅ | `DelayedRefQueue` + `BtrfsRef` model + bounded flush/refcount tracking in `ffs-btrfs::BtrfsExtentAllocator`, with queue/refcount/stress tests. The allocator now also exposes fragmentation-aware largest-free-extent discovery from block-group/extent-tree gaps, and `OpenFs::largest_contiguous_free_run` uses that value for btrfs instead of reporting total free bytes as a conservative upper bound. |

@@ -67,7 +67,7 @@ None. All identified on-disk structures have conformance fixtures.
 | Btrfs chunk-tree items | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover valid multi-stripe chunk-tree item parsing, fixed header truncation, declared stripe payload truncation, zero chunk length, zero stripe length, zero stripes, and multiple RAID profile bit rejection |
 | Btrfs dev items | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover full field-layout parsing, max numeric/classification values, trailing bytes after the fixed 98-byte item, and truncated payload rejection |
 | Btrfs superblocks | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover valid superblock-region parsing, image-offset parsing, bad magic, zero and non-power-of-two sizing fields, oversized sys_chunk_array declarations, and short region/image rejection |
-| Btrfs item payload parsers | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover root/root_ref/inode/dir/xattr/extent payload valid boundaries, multi-entry dir/xattr payloads, short headers, length overflows, unsupported extent types, zero root bytenr rejection, and malformed ROOT_REF fallback behavior for subvolumes/snapshots |
+| Btrfs item payload parsers | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover root/root_ref/inode/dir/xattr/extent payload valid boundaries, multi-entry dir/xattr payloads, short headers, length overflows, unsupported extent types and encoding fields, zero root bytenr rejection, and malformed ROOT_REF fallback behavior for subvolumes/snapshots |
 | Btrfs send streams | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover command CRC32C validation, missing-END rejection, unknown command fallback, zero-length and unknown attributes, multi-attribute commands, trailing partial command headers, and END payload TLV validation |
 | Btrfs delayed refs | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover zero-limit flush preservation, bounded partial flush accounting, delete-underflow queue preservation, and sequence-ordered drain across extent key order |
 | Btrfs transactions | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial mutation tests cover same-tree root replacement, delayed-ref commit failure nonvisibility, and tree-root address overflow nonvisibility |
